page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the t2a server family. It features a single dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical core operating at 3.0 GHz with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations indicate modest performance profiles. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression tests score in the average tier. However, multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and memory bandwidth tests—such as the uncached large-block read at 11.99 GB/sec and write at 7.95 GB/sec—fall into the lower performance percentiles. Redis SET operations achieve 1.27 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.38 GB/sec.

This instance offers a low-cost entry point for ARM64 development and testing. Given its single-core configuration and lower memory bandwidth, it is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, simple microservices, software compilation testing, and low-traffic database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by a single dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 core running at 3.0 GHz. It features 4.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers for multi-core tasks and memory bandwidth, though it achieves average results in single-core stress-ng and single-threaded gzip compression tests. With a single physical core and thread, the t2a-standard-1 represents a highly cost-efficient option for ARM64 development, lightweight web serving, basic microservices, and low-demand utility workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring a 3.0 GHz Neoverse-N1 core and 4 GB of RAM. It is designed for lightweight workloads and cost-efficient ARM development.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-1 is a 1-vCPU ARM64 virtual machine with 4 GB RAM, designed for lightweight utility workloads and ARM development.
tagline: A single-core ARM64 virtual machine with balanced memory for lightweight utility workloads, microservices, and cost-efficient software development.
bullet_points: Single dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 core running at 3.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression performance
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators
Suited for lightweight web serving, microservices, and ARM64 development
